  • 9. Over-Representation Analysis (ORA)

RNAseq Differential Expression Analysis

Dataset: airway | Tools: DESeq2, ComplexHeatmap, EnhancedVolcano, clusterProfiler

Overview

This document walks through a complete RNA-seq differential expression analysis using the airway dataset as a worked example. The contrast of interest is treated (trt) vs untreated (untrt) while accounting for cell-line (cell) as a covariate.

Step Tool Output
Differential expression DESeq2 deseq2_results.csv
Heatmap (top 50 variable genes) ComplexHeatmap inline figure
Volcano plot EnhancedVolcano inline figure
Gene-set enrichment (GSEA) clusterProfiler GSEA() gsea_results.csv, inline figure
Over-representation analysis (ORA) clusterProfiler enricher() ora_results.csv, inline figure

Gene sets for both GSEA and ORA are the CP:KEGG_MEDICUS collection sourced from MSigDB via the msigdbr package.

1. Load libraries

Code 
library(airway)
library(DESeq2)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(EnhancedVolcano)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(msigdbr)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(AnnotationDbi)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(circlize)
library(matrixStats)

2. Configuration

Code 
SEED <- 42

# Colour palettes used throughout the analysis
COL_DEX  <- c("trt" = "#E64B35", "untrt" = "#4DBBD5")
COL_CELL <- c("N061011" = "#00A087", "N052611" = "#3C5488",
              "N080611" = "#F39B7F", "N61311"  = "#8491B4")

3. Load the airway dataset

Code 
data(airway)
se <- airway

4. DESeq2 differential expression analysis

The design formula ~ cell + dex adjusts for cell-line effects while testing for treatment (dex) differences. Genes with fewer than 10 total counts are removed prior to modelling. Log2 fold-changes are shrunk using the ashr method to stabilise estimates for lowly-expressed genes while retaining the Wald statistic for downstream GSEA ranking.

Code 
dds <- DESeqDataSet(se, design = ~ cell + dex)

# Pre-filter: keep only rows with at least 10 reads total
keep <- rowSums(counts(dds)) >= 10
dds  <- dds[keep, ]

dds <- DESeq(dds)

# Extract results: treated vs untreated
# Keep unshrunken results to retain the Wald statistic used for GSEA ranking
res_unshrunk <- results(dds, contrast = c("dex", "trt", "untrt"))
res_shrunk   <- lfcShrink(dds, contrast = c("dex", "trt", "untrt"),
                           res = res_unshrunk, type = "ashr")

res_df <- as.data.frame(res_shrunk) %>%
  rownames_to_column("ensembl_id") %>%
  mutate(stat = as.data.frame(res_unshrunk)[["stat"]])

# Map Ensembl IDs to gene symbols
res_df$symbol <- mapIds(
  org.Hs.eg.db,
  keys    = res_df$ensembl_id,
  keytype = "ENSEMBL",
  column  = "SYMBOL",
  multiVals = "first"
)

# Save full results table
write.csv(res_df, "deseq2_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("DESeq2 analysis complete.\n")
DESeq2 analysis complete.
Code 
cat(sprintf("  Total genes tested: %d\n", nrow(res_df)))
  Total genes tested: 22369
Code 
cat(sprintf("  Significant (padj < 0.05, |LFC| > 1): %d\n",
            sum(!is.na(res_df$padj) & res_df$padj < 0.05 &
                  abs(res_df$log2FoldChange) > 1, na.rm = TRUE)))
  Significant (padj < 0.05, |LFC| > 1): 708

5. Heatmap of top 50 most variable genes

Variance-stabilising transformation (VST) is applied prior to visualisation. The 50 most variable genes are selected, Z-score normalised by row, and displayed with column annotations for treatment group and cell line.

Code 
# Variance-stabilising transformation for visualisation
vsd <- vst(dds, blind = FALSE)

# Select the 50 most variable genes
gene_vars  <- rowVars(assay(vsd))
top50_idx  <- order(gene_vars, decreasing = TRUE)[1:50]
mat        <- assay(vsd)[top50_idx, ]

# Z-score normalise rows
mat <- t(scale(t(mat)))

# Add gene symbols as row labels (fall back to Ensembl ID if symbol is NA)
row_symbols <- mapIds(
  org.Hs.eg.db,
  keys    = rownames(mat),
  keytype = "ENSEMBL",
  column  = "SYMBOL",
  multiVals = "first"
)
rownames(mat) <- ifelse(is.na(row_symbols), rownames(mat), row_symbols)

# Column annotations
col_df  <- as.data.frame(colData(dds)[, c("dex", "cell")])

# Define colour palette for the heatmap
heatmap_colors <- colorRampPalette(c("#4DBBD5", "white", "#E64B35"))(100)

# ComplexHeatmap exports a pheatmap() compatibility wrapper that returns a
# Heatmap-class object, which is then drawn with draw() below.
heatmap_plot <- pheatmap(
  mat,
  color                    = heatmap_colors,
  annotation_col           = col_df,
  annotation_colors        = list(dex = COL_DEX, cell = COL_CELL),
  show_colnames            = TRUE,
  show_rownames            = TRUE,
  fontsize_row             = 8,
  clustering_distance_rows = "euclidean",
  clustering_method        = "complete",
  main                     = "Top 50 Most Variable Genes (airway)"
)

draw(heatmap_plot)

6. Volcano plot

The volcano plot visualises shrunken log2 fold-changes against adjusted p-values. The top 10 up-regulated and top 10 down-regulated significant genes (padj < 0.05) are labelled.

Code 
res_volcano <- res_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(symbol)) %>%
  distinct(symbol, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  column_to_rownames("symbol")

sig_genes <- res_volcano %>%
  filter(!is.na(padj) & padj < 0.05 & !is.na(log2FoldChange))

top_up   <- sig_genes %>% arrange(desc(log2FoldChange)) %>% head(10)
top_down <- sig_genes %>% arrange(log2FoldChange)       %>% head(10)

genes_to_label <- unique(c(rownames(top_up), rownames(top_down)))

EnhancedVolcano(
  res_volcano,
  lab        = rownames(res_volcano),
  x          = "log2FoldChange",
  y          = "padj",
  title      = "Treated vs Untreated (airway)",
  subtitle   = "DESeq2 with LFC shrinkage (ashr)",
  pCutoff    = 0.05,
  FCcutoff   = 1,
  pointSize  = 2,
  labSize    = 3,
  col        = c("grey30", "#4DBBD5", "#00A087", "#E64B35"),
  legendLabels = c("NS", "Log2FC", "p-value", "p-value & Log2FC"),
  drawConnectors = TRUE,
  widthConnectors = 0.5,
  selectLab  = genes_to_label
)

7. Build gene sets from MSigDB (CP:KEGG_MEDICUS)

Gene sets are retrieved from MSigDB via msigdbr. The CP:KEGG_MEDICUS collection (MSigDB C2 category) is used for both GSEA and ORA. To use a different collection (e.g. Hallmark gene sets), simply change the category / subcollection arguments — explore the full MSigDB database at https://www.gsea-msigdb.org/gsea/msigdb.

Code 
kegg_medicus <- msigdbr(
  species    = "Homo sapiens",
  category   = "C2",
  subcollection = "CP:KEGG_MEDICUS"
)

TERM2GENE <- kegg_medicus %>%
  select(gs_id, gene_symbol) %>%
  as.data.frame()

TERM2NAME <- kegg_medicus %>%
  select(gs_id, gs_name) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  as.data.frame()

# These cleaning up steps will not be necessary if you switch to something other than KEGG_MEDICUS
TERM2NAME$gs_name <- gsub("KEGG_MEDICUS_REFERENCE_", "", TERM2NAME$gs_name)
TERM2NAME$gs_name <- gsub("KEGG_MEDICUS_", "", TERM2NAME$gs_name)

cat(sprintf("CP:KEGG_MEDICUS gene sets loaded: %d pathways, %d gene-set associations\n",
            nrow(TERM2NAME), nrow(TERM2GENE)))
CP:KEGG_MEDICUS gene sets loaded: 658 pathways, 9688 gene-set associations

8. Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA)

Genes are ranked by their DESeq2 Wald statistic (sign encodes direction; magnitude encodes confidence). One entry per gene symbol is kept (highest |stat|). The “stat” column in DESeq2 represents the test statistic for each gene, calculated as the log2 fold change divided by the standard error of the log2 fold change (lfcSE).

Code 
# Build a ranked gene list using the DESeq2 Wald statistic.
# Use genes with valid symbols only; keep one entry per symbol (highest |stat|).
ranked_df <- res_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(symbol), !is.na(stat)) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(stat))) %>%
  distinct(symbol, .keep_all = TRUE)

gene_list        <- ranked_df$stat
names(gene_list) <- ranked_df$symbol
gene_list        <- sort(gene_list, decreasing = TRUE)

set.seed(SEED)
gsea_result <- GSEA(
  geneList     = gene_list,
  TERM2GENE    = TERM2GENE,
  TERM2NAME    = TERM2NAME,
  minGSSize    = 10,
  maxGSSize    = 500,
  pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
  eps          = 0,
  seed         = SEED,
  verbose      = FALSE
)

gsea_df <- as.data.frame(gsea_result)
write.csv(gsea_df, "gsea_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat(sprintf("GSEA complete. Significant pathways (padj < 0.05): %d\n",
            nrow(gsea_df)))
GSEA complete. Significant pathways (padj < 0.05): 40
Code 
if (nrow(gsea_df) > 0) {
  print(dotplot(gsea_result, showCategory = 20, title = "GSEA – CP:KEGG_MEDICUS"))
}

9. Over-Representation Analysis (ORA)

ORA tests whether significantly differentially expressed genes (padj < 0.05, |LFC| > 1) are over-represented in any gene set relative to all tested genes with a valid symbol as the background universe.

Code 
# Significant DE genes: padj < 0.05 and |LFC| > 1
sig_genes <- res_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(padj), padj < 0.05, abs(log2FoldChange) > 1,
         !is.na(symbol)) %>%
  distinct(symbol) %>%
  pull(symbol)

cat(sprintf("ORA input: %d significant DE genes\n", length(sig_genes)))
ORA input: 665 significant DE genes
Code 
# Background = all tested genes with a valid symbol
universe <- res_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(symbol)) %>%
  distinct(symbol) %>%
  pull(symbol)

ora_result <- enricher(
  gene         = sig_genes,
  universe     = universe,
  TERM2GENE    = TERM2GENE,
  TERM2NAME    = TERM2NAME,
  minGSSize    = 10,
  maxGSSize    = 500,
  pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
  qvalueCutoff = 0.2
)

ora_df <- as.data.frame(ora_result)
write.csv(ora_df, "ora_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat(sprintf("ORA complete. Significant pathways (padj < 0.05): %d\n",
            nrow(ora_df)))
ORA complete. Significant pathways (padj < 0.05): 1
Code 
if (nrow(ora_df) > 0) {
  print(dotplot(ora_result, showCategory = 20, title = "ORA – CP:KEGG_MEDICUS"))
}
Code 
cat("\nAll analyses complete.\n")

All analyses complete.
